financial.note: PTT holds ~45-48% stake; TOP serves as the group's primary refining flagship.
Holds ~13.7% stake; indicative of high foreign institutional interest and liquidity.
Holds ~3.76% stake; provides capital stability and alignment with government investment strategy.

supply_chain.note: Refining margins are highly sensitive to crude premiums and light-heavy differentials. The Clean Fuel Project (CFP) allows for heavier, cheaper crude processing.
Operations successfully resumed in early 2026, eliminating USD 0.5/bbl in temporary ship-to-ship transfer costs.
Primary off-taker for refined products (gasoline/diesel) in the domestic market.

equity_cross_holdings.note: Provides vertical integration into utility supply for refinery operations.

competitors.note: Direct rival in complex refining; both compete for market share in middle distillates (diesel/jet fuel).
Primary rival in the domestic refining and retail sector, especially post-BCP’s Esso acquisition.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The primary driver of core profit; 1Q26 GRM is strong at ~$6.8/bbl due to diesel and jet fuel demand.
Project is 97%+ complete as of April 2026; early completion in 2Q28 targets a refining capacity of 400,000 bpd.
As a USD-earner, THB depreciation acts as a tailwind for translated revenue and inventory valuations.
Sharp price drops result in stock losses, while range-bound prices favor refinery crack spreads.
